sections.title: When right of private defence of property extends to causing death
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: The encoding drops canonical limbs `(b)(i)`, `(b)(ii)`, and `(2)(a)` to `(2)(f)`, collapsing subsection `(2)` into a definition string and failing to preserve the statute's disjunctive structure.
sections.suggested_fix: Re-encode subsection `(1)(b)` and subsection `(2)` with explicit nested items or `any_of` branches so every canonical limb is represented verbatim.

sections.title: When such right extends to causing any harm other than death
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is tagged `[15/2019]` but the encoding uses `effective 2019-12-31`, while repo-local precedent for Act 15 of 2019 commencement is `2020-01-01`, so the effective-date encoding is not sane.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the later effective date with `2020-01-01` if that is the intended commencement date for this provision.

sections.title: Abetment outside Singapore of an offence in Singapore
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoding uses only `effective 1872-01-01`, but section 108B was introduced by Act 51 of 2007, which commenced on 2008-02-01, so the effective-date encoding is not sane.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace or supplement the generic commencement with an `effective 2008-02-01` clause after confirming the section’s amendment history.

sections.title: Liability of abettor for an offence caused by the act abetted different from that intended by the abettor
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The canonical text is a liability attribution rule and does not itself prescribe a standalone punishment clause, but the encoding adds a `penalty {}` block.
sections.suggested_fix: Move the "liable for the effect caused" language out of `penalty {}` into a non-penalty liability/refinement construct while preserving the illustration verbatim.

sections.title: Abetment of offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: The statute’s disjunctive penalty phrase "liable to fine or to caning" is not fully captured structurally because the encoding machine-encodes only `fine := unlimited` and leaves caning only in supplementary text.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode the additional liability as a disjunctive penalty structure that preserves both fine and caning as separate machine-readable alternatives.

sections.title: Offences against authority
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoded statute uses `effective 2020-01-01` for a section marked `[15/2019]`, and I could not verify that as the correct amendment commencement date for section 121B.
sections.suggested_fix: Verify the actual commencement date for the Act 15 of 2019 amendment affecting section 121B and replace the current `effective` date if needed.

sections.title: Assaulting President, etc., with intent to compel or restrain the exercise of any lawful power
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: The encoding does not machine-encode the statute's express life-imprisonment alternative and the later `effective 2019-12-31` date is not reliable enough to certify for a section marked `[15/2019]`.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode life imprisonment as an explicit penalty branch and confirm the correct commencement date for the `[15/2019]` amendment before restamping.

sections.title: “Harbour”
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoding uses only `effective 1872-01-01`, but section 130A appears to have been introduced later and external consolidation history shows `[51/2007]`.
sections.suggested_fix: Add an effective date reflecting the amending Act that introduced section 130A, then re-run L3 review.

sections.title: Piracy by law of nations. Cf. 12 and 13 Victoria c. 96 (Admiralty Offences (Colonial) Act 1849)
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: Section 130B is a later inserted section number, but the encoding uses only `effective 1872-01-01`, so the effective date is not sane on the present record.
sections.suggested_fix: Verify the insertion commencement date from Singapore Statutes Online legislative history and add that later effective date before stamping.

sections.title: Piratical acts
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoding uses only `effective 1872-01-01`, but section 130C is a later inserted provision and therefore needs an amending-act effective date rather than the generic base date alone.
sections.suggested_fix: Add an effective date for the amendment that introduced section 130C, then re-run L3 review.

sections.title: Punishment for genocide
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The canonical limb `(b)` authorises imprisonment for life or up to 20 years, but the encoding’s operative penalty only models `imprisonment := 0 years .. 20 years` and leaves life imprisonment only in supplementary text.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode the limb `(b)` punishment so the executable penalty model expressly captures the life-imprisonment alternative as well as the term sentence.

sections.title: Aiding escape of, rescuing, or harbouring such prisoner
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The penalty encoding omits life imprisonment as an actual encoded penalty branch and makes fine unconditional even though the statute allows fine only if the offender is not sentenced to imprisonment for life.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode the life-imprisonment alternative structurally and make the fine apply only on the non-life-imprisonment branch.

sections.title: Abetment of mutiny, if mutiny is committed in consequence thereof
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The encoding does not machine-encode the statute's express life-imprisonment alternative, leaving it only in `supplementary` text, so the penalty facts are incomplete.
sections.suggested_fix: Add an explicit life-imprisonment penalty branch or field alongside death and the term-imprisonment branch, while keeping the existing conditional fine logic.

sections.title: Deserter concealed on board merchant vessel through negligence of master
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is tagged `[15/2019]` but the encoding uses `effective 2019-12-31`, while repo-local L3 precedent treats Act 15 of 2019 commencement as `2020-01-01`, so the effective-date encoding is not sane.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the later effective date with `2020-01-01` if that is the intended commencement date for this provision.

sections.title: Saving
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: The canonical section is a single conditional saving rule, but the encoding reduces it to three definitions and does not preserve the statute's operative logical structure.
sections.suggested_fix: Re-encode the section as an explicit conditional rule that captures the "where ... no person ... shall be subject" logic rather than as standalone definitions.

sections.title: Rioting, armed with a deadly weapon
sections.rationale: illustration count needs recounting
sections.reason: The canonical section includes an Illustration heading with a preserved cross-reference note, but the encoding keeps that text only in comments instead of as a separate `illustration` block.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the comment-only preservation with an explicit `illustration` block containing the canonical text verbatim.

sections.title: Owner or occupier of land on which an unlawful assembly is held
sections.rationale: raw uses 'or' but encoding uses all_of without any_of — switch element grouping to any_of.
sections.reason: The encoding collapses the prevention and dispersal/suppression omission limbs into one clause and drops the second canonical "do not", changing the section's conjunctive logic.
sections.suggested_fix: Split the omission requirements so the notice, prevention, and dispersal/suppression failures are each preserved with the same negated conjunctions as the canonical text.

sections.title: Liability of person for whose benefit a riot is committed
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: The encoding decouples who had reason to believe from who failed to use lawful means, which broadens the statutory logic in a way the English text does not clearly permit.
sections.suggested_fix: Model the person and the agent-or-manager as correlated alternative branches so each branch carries both the foresight and failure-to-act condition together.

sections.title: Harbouring persons hired for an unlawful assembly
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoded statute uses `effective 2019-12-31` for a provision marked `[15/2019]`, and I could not certify that as the correct commencement date for the section.
sections.suggested_fix: Confirm the actual commencement date for the section 157 amendment under Act 15 of 2019 and replace the unsupported effective date before restamping.

sections.title: Being hired to take part in an unlawful assembly or riot
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The `base_offence` condition is broad enough that an armed offender also satisfies it, so the encoding can trigger both the 2-year and 5-year penalty branches even though section 158 states alternative punishments for separate limbs.
sections.suggested_fix: Make the penalty triggers mutually exclusive by separating the ordinary limb from the armed limb at the element or condition level.

sections.title: Public servant disobeying a direction of the law, with intent to cause injury to any person
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is tagged `[Act 25 of 2021 wef 01/04/2022]` but the statute header encodes only `effective 1872-01-01`, so the later operative date is missing.
sections.suggested_fix: Add `effective 2022-04-01` to the statute header while preserving the original `effective 1872-01-01`.

sections.title: Absconding to avoid arrest on warrant or service of summons, etc., proceeding from a public servant
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: The encoding makes every case depend on `ordinary_process` or `court_process`, so absconding to avoid arrest on a warrant is not faithfully preserved as a standalone route to the base penalty.
sections.suggested_fix: Model warrant-avoidance separately from summons/notice/order service, and apply the enhanced branch only where the summons, notice or order is for court attendance or production.

sections.title: Omission to produce document or electronic record to public servant by person legally bound to produce such document or electronic record
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is tagged `[15/2019]` but the encoding uses `effective 2019-12-31`, while repo-local precedent for Act 15 of 2019 commencement is `2020-01-01`, so the effective-date encoding is not sane.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the later effective date with `2020-01-01` if that is the intended commencement date for this provision.

sections.title: Refusing to answer public servant authorised to question
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is tagged `[15/2019]` but the encoding uses only `effective 1872-01-01`, so the effective-date encoding is not sane.
sections.suggested_fix: Add the verified commencement date for the `[15/2019]` amendment as an additional `effective` clause before restamping.

sections.title: Resistance to taking of property by lawful authority of public servant
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoded section is tagged with amendment `[15/2019]` but lists only `effective 1872-01-01`, so it omits a later amendment effective date required by the checklist.
sections.suggested_fix: Add the applicable later effective date for the 2019 amendment to the statute header, consistent with the canonical amendment history and nearby amended sections.

sections.title: Illegal purchase or bid for property offered for sale by authority of public servant
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: The encoding factors the offence into `purchase_or_bid` plus an alternative mens rea block, which incorrectly makes a purchase without intent to perform punishable even though the canonical second limb applies only to a bid.
sections.suggested_fix: Restructure the elements as two disjunctive branches: `(purchase or bid) + knowledge of legal incapacity`, and `bid + no intention to perform`.

sections.title: Obstructing public servant in discharge of his public functions
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoding uses `effective 2019-12-31` for the `[15/2019]` amendment, but repo-local precedent for comparable Penal Code sections points to `2020-01-01`, so the effective-date encoding is not sane enough to stamp.
sections.suggested_fix: Confirm the actual commencement date for the Act 15 of 2019 amendment to section 186 and update the `effective` clause accordingly before restamping.

sections.title: Threat of injury to induce any person to refrain from applying for protection to a public servant
sections.rationale: raw uses 'or' but encoding uses all_of without any_of — switch element grouping to any_of.
sections.reason: The `elements` split changes the statutory meaning by encoding the application as being for protection against injury to the public servant, instead of an application made to a legally empowered public servant for protection against injury.
sections.suggested_fix: Re-encode the application/public-servant relationship so the offence matches the canonical sentence structure without changing who the protection is sought from.

sections.title: Giving or fabricating false evidence with intent to procure conviction of a capital offence
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The penalty encoding does not faithfully capture the statute because imprisonment for life is only mentioned in supplementary text and the aggravated death-eligible branch is encoded broadly enough to cover fabrication as well as giving false evidence.
sections.suggested_fix: Model the base punishment as life imprisonment or up to 20 years with fine only on the non-life branch, and scope the aggravated branch to the person who gives such false evidence.

sections.title: Bribery of witnesses
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoding uses only `effective 1872-01-01` for a lettered section `204B`, so the effective-date sanity check cannot be certified without confirming the later insertion date from source history.
sections.suggested_fix: Confirm the actual commencement date for section 204B from SSO or legislative history and add it as an `effective` clause if the section was introduced later.

sections.title: Destruction of document or electronic record to prevent its production as evidence
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: The encoding rewrites the canonical sentence into `all_of` plus two `any_of` groups in a way that appears to make the prevention intent mandatory and treat lawful summons as a sibling alternative circumstance, which is not clearly faithful to the statute's original connective structure.
sections.suggested_fix: Re-encode the operative clause more literally, preserving the placement of the final `or after he has been lawfully summoned or required...` limb before seeking L3 stamp.

sections.title: Fraudulent claim to property to prevent its seizure as a forfeiture, in execution of a decree or under or pursuant to an enforcement order
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: The encoding places `deceptive_practice` inside the same `all_of` bundle as `no_right_or_rightful_claim`, but the statute reads as a disjunctive branch where that knowledge qualifier attaches to accepting/receiving/claiming property, not necessarily to practising deception.
sections.suggested_fix: Restructure the elements so the act branches track the statute’s syntax, with the knowledge qualifier scoped only to the acceptance/receipt/claim branch and the common seizure-prevention intent applied to both branches.

sections.title: False charge of offence made with intent to injure
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: The encoding makes the serious-charge qualifier a required `elements` branch and fabricates a mutually exclusive `ordinary_false_charge` condition, instead of modeling the statute's base offence with an aggravated penalty condition.
sections.suggested_fix: Keep the offence elements to the canonical charging conduct and knowledge/intent only, then express the 2-year base penalty and the 7-year serious-charge branch as faithful sibling penalty conditions.

sections.title: Public servant disobeying a direction of law with intent to save person from punishment or property from forfeiture
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: The `.yh` uses an `all_of` structure in the person-saving limb that turns “save any person from legal punishment, or subject him to a lesser punishment” into a required sibling element instead of preserving the statute’s disjunctive object of the intent/knowledge clause.
sections.suggested_fix: Re-encode the person-facing limb so the “save from legal punishment” and “subject to a lesser punishment” alternatives remain within the same disjunctive intent/knowledge branch.

sections.title: Intentional omission to apprehend on the part of a public servant bound by law to apprehend person under sentence of a court of justice
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The first penalty branch does not faithfully capture the statute because imprisonment for life is only preserved in supplementary text rather than in the operative penalty encoding.
sections.suggested_fix: Re-encode limb (a) so the life-imprisonment alternative is represented explicitly rather than only described in supplementary prose.

sections.title: Having possession of a counterfeit Government stamp
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: The canonical penalty is cumulative ("shall be punished with imprisonment ... and shall also be liable to fine"), but the encoding uses `penalty or_both`, which incorrectly makes imprisonment optional.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the `penalty or_both` structure with a cumulative/default penalty form that preserves mandatory imprisonment plus liability to an unlimited fine.

sections.title: Punishment for committing affray
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoding uses only `effective 1872-01-01`, but section 267B is an inserted later section and I could not verify a sane later commencement date from the local sources.
sections.suggested_fix: Confirm the actual insertion commencement date from Singapore Statutes Online legislative history and add it as an `effective` clause before restamping.

sections.title: “Intentionally”
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: Canonical subsection (2) contains two disjunctive limbs, `(2)(a)` and `(2)(b)`, but the encoding stops after the em dash and does not represent either branch.
sections.suggested_fix: Preserve subsection (2)(a) and (2)(b) as explicit alternative branches under subsection (2), with the canonical text verbatim.

sections.title: “Knowingly”
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoding omits the earlier commencement date for material tagged `[15/2019]` and only records `effective 2020-02-10`, so the effective-date history is incomplete.
sections.suggested_fix: Add the missing amendment commencement date (likely `2019-12-31`) alongside `2020-02-10` after confirming it against the section’s amendment history.

sections.title: Rash or negligent conduct with respect to any machinery in possession or under charge of offender
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The encoding drops the four conditional punishment limbs in canonical subsection (3) and incorrectly states that the raw text stops at the dash.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode subsection (3)(a) to (d) as conditional penalty branches with the correct imprisonment and fine consequences, including the $5,000 fine cap only for subsection (1)(a) or (b).

sections.title: Possession, distribution, etc., of child sex-doll
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is tagged `[15/2019]` but the encoding uses `effective 2019-12-31`, while repo-local L3 precedent treats Act 15 of 2019 commencement as `2020-01-01`, so the effective-date encoding is not sane enough to stamp.
sections.suggested_fix: Verify the commencement date for section 292A under Act 15 of 2019 and replace the later `effective` clause with the confirmed date before restamping.

sections.title: Obscene acts
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The statute is tagged with amendment marker `[15/2019]` but the encoding uses only `effective 1872-01-01`, which fails the amended-section effective-date requirement.
sections.suggested_fix: Add the amendment commencement date as an `effective` clause alongside any historical baseline date if the section remained in force through amendment.

sections.title: “Electronic record”
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoding uses only `effective 1872-01-01`, but section 29B was introduced later, with external legislative history indicating Act 51 of 2007 commenced on 1 February 2008.
sections.suggested_fix: Add the later commencement date as an `effective` clause after confirming the source amendment metadata for section 29B.

sections.title: Culpable homicide by causing the death of a person other than the person whose death was intended
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is marked `[15/2019]` but the encoding uses `effective 2019-12-31`, while the Criminal Law Reform Act 2019 (Act 15 of 2019) entered into force on 2020-01-01, so the effective-date encoding is not sane enough to stamp.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the later `effective` clause with the verified commencement date `2020-01-01`, then rerun L3 review.

sections.title: Causing death of child below 14 years of age, domestic worker or vulnerable person by sustained abuse
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: The encoding leaves subsection (2) empty even though the canonical statute contains definitional content there, and its penalty encodes fine but omits the statute's separate caning liability.
sections.suggested_fix: Populate subsection (2) with the canonical definitions and add the missing caning branch to the cumulative penalty structure without changing the source text.

sections.title: Causing death in furtherance of group’s object
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: Subsection (1)(b) is not faithfully encoded because the canonical disjunction between items (b)(i) and (b)(ii) is replaced by the truncated placeholder `paragraph_b_truncated := "Knowing that —"` and the two alternatives are omitted.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode subsection (1) so paragraph (b) contains an explicit `any_of` that preserves both canonical alternatives in items (b)(i) and (b)(ii).

sections.title: Attempt to commit culpable homicide
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The `hurt_caused` penalty branch omits a structured caning punishment even though the canonical text expressly provides imprisonment, fine, caning, or any combination of those punishments.
sections.suggested_fix: Add the missing `caning :=` punishment to the aggravated `when hurt_caused` penalty branch so every canonical punishment option is encoded.

sections.title: Punishment for infanticide
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section carries amendment markers `[15/2019]` and `[Act 23 of 2021 wef 01/03/2022]` but the encoding uses only `effective 1872-01-01`, so the effective-date encoding is not sane enough to stamp.
sections.suggested_fix: Add later `effective` clauses for the post-1872 amendments, including `2022-03-01` and the verified commencement date for Act 15 of 2019, then rerun L3 review.

sections.title: Causing miscarriage
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: The encoding invents a `pregnancy_not_more_than_16_weeks` condition and makes pregnancy duration an element-level `any_of`, but the statute states a base offence with a higher penalty only if the pregnancy exceeds 16 weeks.
sections.suggested_fix: Keep the miscarriage elements unconditional and model the `more than 16 weeks` language only as an aggravated penalty branch, with the base penalty as the default.

sections.title: Death caused by act done with intent to cause miscarriage
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The encoding does not faithfully capture the no-consent penalty alternative because it omits an explicit life-imprisonment branch and invents a separate `woman_without_consent_non_life_sentence` condition.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode the no-consent punishment as explicit alternatives of imprisonment for life or the above-mentioned imprisonment-plus-fine punishment using the canonical condition only.

sections.title: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt which causes grievous hurt
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is marked `[15/2019]` but the encoding uses `effective 2019-12-31`, while repo-local L3 precedent for Act 15 of 2019 commencement is `2020-01-01`, so the effective-date encoding is not sane enough to stamp.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the later `effective` clause with the verified commencement date `2020-01-01`, then rerun L3 review.

sections.title: Voluntarily causing hurt by dangerous weapons or means
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The encoding fabricates a caning range of `0 .. 24 strokes` even though the canonical text states only liability to caning without any numeric stroke count.
sections.suggested_fix: Preserve the caning limb without inventing a stroke count, such as by moving the verbatim caning language into a supplementary penalty field.

sections.title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapons or means
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The encoding does not faithfully capture the punishment because it omits an explicit life-imprisonment branch and invents a separate `non_life_sentence` condition for fine liability.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode the penalty as the canonical alternatives and conditional fine consequence without introducing a non-canonical condition.

sections.title: Voluntarily causing hurt to extort property or to constrain to an illegal act
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The encoding fabricates a caning range of `0 .. 24 strokes` even though the canonical text only says the offender is liable "to caning" without specifying any stroke count.
sections.suggested_fix: Remove the invented numeric caning clause and preserve the caning limb only in an explicitly labelled textual refinement or supplementary punishment text that does not add a number.

sections.title: Causing hurt by means of poison, etc., with intent to commit an offence
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The encoding hard-codes `caning := 0 .. 24 strokes` even though the canonical text only says the offender is liable to caning without specifying any stroke count.
sections.suggested_fix: Preserve the caning limb without inventing a numeric range, consistent with the repo's handling of uncapped caning provisions.

sections.title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt to extort property, or to constrain to an illegal act
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The encoding hard-codes `caning := 0 .. 24 strokes` even though the canonical text only says the offender is liable to caning without specifying any stroke count.
sections.suggested_fix: Preserve the caning limb without inventing a numeric range, consistent with the repo's handling of uncapped caning provisions.

sections.title: Voluntarily causing hurt to extort confession or to compel restoration of property
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The penalty encoding is not faithful because it makes `fine` part of a cumulative penalty and omits a structured `caning` branch, even though the canonical text says the offender is liable to fine or to caning.
sections.suggested_fix: Model the punishment like the comparable hurt/grievous-hurt extortion sections, with imprisonment plus an alternative branch for `fine` or `caning`.

sections.title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt to extort confession or to compel restoration of property
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The encoding omits a structured caning punishment even though the canonical text expressly makes the offender liable to caning in addition to imprisonment and fine.
sections.suggested_fix: Add an explicit caning limb, using `caning := unspecified;` if needed, so every canonical punishment option is encoded without inventing a stroke count.

sections.title: Voluntarily causing hurt to deter public servant from his duty
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The encoding omits a structured caning punishment even though the canonical text expressly makes the offender liable to fine or to caning, and the grammar now supports `caning := unspecified` for that case.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the prose-only preservation of the caning limb with an explicit structured caning branch so every canonical punishment option is encoded without inventing a stroke count.

sections.title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt to deter public servant from his duty
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The canonical punishment includes liability to fine or to caning, but the encoding only models `fine := unlimited` and omits any `caning :=` clause.
sections.suggested_fix: Add an explicit caning branch to the penalty block so the encoded punishment captures both alternatives stated in the statute.

sections.title: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt on provocation which causes grievous hurt
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is marked `[15/2019]` but the encoding uses `effective 2019-12-31`, while repo-local L3 precedent treats Act 15 of 2019 commencement as `2020-01-01`, so the effective-date encoding is not sane enough to stamp.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the later `effective` clause with `2020-01-01`, then rerun L3 review.

sections.title: Voluntarily causing hurt on provocation
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section carries amendment marker `[15/2019]` but the encoding uses `effective 2019-12-31`, while the Criminal Law Reform Act 2019 commenced on `2020-01-01`, so the effective-date encoding is not sane enough to stamp.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the later effective date with `2020-01-01` and re-run L3 review.

sections.title: Punishment for wrongful confinement
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoding is tagged with amendment marker `[15/2019]` but uses `effective 2019-12-31`, while Act 15 of 2019 commenced on 2020-01-01.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the later effective date with `2020-01-01` and keep the original `1872-01-01` commencement clause.

sections.title: Punishment for abduction
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: The encoding omits a structured caning punishment even though the canonical text expressly includes caning, and `penalty or_both` does not faithfully capture a three-way “fine, or caning, or any combination” punishment clause.
sections.suggested_fix: Add an explicit caning limb without inventing a stroke count and re-express the penalty logic so all three punishment options and their combinations are structurally captured.

sections.title: Kidnapping or abducting with intent secretly and wrongfully to confine a person
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The encoding fabricates `caning := 0 .. 24 strokes` even though the canonical text states only that the offender is liable "to caning" without any numeric stroke count.
sections.suggested_fix: Remove the invented stroke range and preserve the caning limb without adding a number of strokes.

sections.title: Kidnapping or abducting a woman to compel her marriage, etc.
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The encoding fabricates `caning := 0 .. 24 strokes` even though the canonical text only makes the offender liable to caning without specifying any numeric stroke count.
sections.suggested_fix: Preserve the caning limb without inventing a number of strokes, and keep the imprisonment-plus-fine-or-caning structure faithful to the statute.

sections.title: Kidnapping or abducting in order to subject a person to grievous hurt, slavery, etc.
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is marked `[15/2019]` but the encoding uses `effective 2019-12-31`, while repo-local L3 precedent for Act 15 of 2019 commencement is `2020-01-01`, so the effective-date encoding is not sane enough to stamp.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the amendment effective date with the correct commencement date for Act 15 of 2019, then re-run L3 review.

sections.title: Wrongfully concealing or keeping in confinement a kidnapped person
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: The encoding uses top-level `all_of` with freeform strings for "kidnapped or abducted" and "conceals or keeps such person in confinement" instead of preserving those disjunctive alternatives as structured `any_of` branches.
sections.suggested_fix: Split the knowledge and actus reus limbs into explicit `any_of` alternatives while keeping the overall offence conjunctive.

sections.title: Exploitative sexual penetration of minor of or above 16 but below 18 years of age
sections.rationale: subsection structure needs comparison
sections.reason: Canonical subsection (2) contains operative consent-related text, but the encoded `subsection (2)` block is empty and therefore does not faithfully preserve the statute.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode subsection (2)(a) and (2)(b) explicitly within `subsection (2)` using the same structured preservation standard as the neighboring Penal Code sections.

sections.title: Commercial sex with minor below 18 years of age outside Singapore
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoded statute uses `effective 2020-01-01` even though comparable Penal Code sections introduced by the same `[15/2019]` amendment in this codebase use `2019-12-31`, so the amendment effective date is not sane enough to stamp.
sections.suggested_fix: Verify the commencement date for Act 15 of 2019 for section 376C and update the `effective` clause to the correct date before rerunning L3 review.

sections.title: Exploitative sexual grooming of minor of or above 16 but below 18 years of age
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: The encoding truncates subsection (1) after paragraph (b)'s opening words and collapses subsection (2) without preserving the operative paragraph structure and English conjunctions in the canonical text.
sections.suggested_fix: Restore subsection (1)(b)(i)-(iv) and subsection (2)(a)-(c) as structured Yuho elements with the correct conjunctive/disjunctive logic.

sections.title: Sexual communication with minor below 16 years of age
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: Subsections (2) and (3) are not faithfully preserved because the encoded file keeps only opening text and omits subsection (2)(a)-(b) plus both conditional penalty limbs in subsection (3)(a)-(b).
sections.suggested_fix: Encode subsection (2)(a)-(b) explicitly and represent subsection (3) with both conditional penalty branches, preserving the original conditions and "or with both" structure.

sections.title: Exploitative sexual activity or image in presence of minor of or above 16 but below 18 years of age
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: Subsection (2) is left empty and subsection (1) omits canonical sub-paragraphs (c)(i) to (c)(iii), and the section uses `effective 2019-12-31` for `[15/2019]` where repo-local L3 precedent treats the Act 15 of 2019 commencement as `2020-01-01`.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode all canonical subsection content, including subsection (2) and paragraph (c)(i)-(iii), and verify the Act 15 of 2019 commencement date before rerunning L3 review.

sections.title: Sexual grooming of minor below 16 years of age
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: The encoding omits subsection (1)(b)(i)-(iii), subsection (2)(a)-(c), and the subsection (4) conditional punishment branches, so the statute's operative conjunctive conditions and `or with both` penalty structure are not faithfully represented.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode the missing sub-items explicitly and represent subsection (4) with both age-based penalty branches, including imprisonment, fine, and `or_both`.

sections.title: Distribution of voyeuristic image or recording
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: Canonical subsection (2) is missing from the encoding, and subsections (3) and (4) omit or mis-encode the caning and alternative/cumulative penalty structure stated in the statute.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode subsection (2) verbatim from `sub_items`, add the missing caning liability, and model subsection (4) as imprisonment plus alternative fine-or-caning rather than a single imprisonment-plus-fine block.

sections.title: Sexual exposure
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: Subsection (4) says the offender "shall also be liable to fine or to caning", but the encoding only models fine structurally and leaves caning as supplementary prose.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the freeform supplementary sentence in subsection (4) with a structured penalty branch that explicitly encodes caning as an alternative to fine.

sections.title: Producing child abuse material
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: Subsection (3)'s canonical itemised production methods in (a)-(c) are omitted, and subsection (2)'s penalty omits the statute's express caning alternative.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode subsection (3)(a)-(c) explicitly and represent subsection (2) as mandatory imprisonment plus the disjunctive fine-or-caning liability without inventing any further caning details.

sections.title: Distributing or selling child abuse material
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: Subsection (2) says imprisonment plus liability to fine or to caning, but the encoding models only the fine branch structurally and leaves the caning branch in supplementary text.
sections.suggested_fix: Add a separate caning-liability penalty branch, as used in nearby section encodings, without inventing any stroke count.

sections.title: Advertising or seeking child abuse material
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The canonical punishment makes the offender liable to fine or to caning, but the encoding only structures the fine limb and leaves caning in prose instead of an explicit punishment clause.
sections.suggested_fix: Add an explicit caning branch, using `caning := unspecified` if needed, so every canonical punishment option is encoded without inventing a stroke count.

sections.title: Possession of or gaining access to child abuse material
sections.rationale: subsection structure needs comparison
sections.reason: The encoding includes subsection (3) as a shell with only an illustration, but the canonical source also contains substantive paragraph (3)(a) and (3)(b) text that is omitted.
sections.suggested_fix: Add the omitted subsection (3)(a) and (3)(b) definitions as structured content under subsection (3) while preserving the existing illustration verbatim.

sections.title: Sexual penetration with living animal
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: The encoding leaves canonical subsection (3) empty, fabricates a `0 .. 24 strokes` caning cap for subsection (4) where the statute states only that the offender is liable to caning, and uses only `effective 1872-01-01` even though section 377B was introduced by Act 51 of 2007 with commencement on 1 February 2008.
sections.suggested_fix: Populate subsection (3) verbatim from the canonical text, change subsection (4) caning to an unspecified liability form, and add the 2008 commencement date to the section’s effective clauses.

sections.title: Meaning of exploitative relationship
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: Subsection (2) omits the canonical paragraph list in `(2)(a)` to `(2)(g)`, and the operative list logic in subsections `(1)` and `(2)` is flattened into `definitions` instead of being encoded as structured conjunctive/disjunctive content.
sections.suggested_fix: Preserve subsection `(1)`'s required factors and subsection `(2)`'s seven presumed relationships as structured content, using list logic that matches the statute’s English.

sections.title: Interpretation of sections 375 to 377BO (sexual offences)
sections.rationale: subsection structure needs comparison
sections.reason: The encoding does not preserve the canonical subsection content because subsection (3) omits items (a) to (f)(iii) and subsection (1) leaves several definitions as incomplete stubs rather than carrying the full statutory text.
sections.suggested_fix: Re-encode subsection (1) and subsection (3) so every canonical definition limb and paragraph in `act.json` is represented explicitly in `statute.yh`.

sections.title: Punishment for theft of a motor vehicle
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: Subsection (3) is reduced to a comment instead of preserving the canonical definitions of “component part” and “motor vehicle”, and the encoding uses only `effective 1872-01-01` even though this section has later amendment provenance.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode subsection (3) with the two statutory definitions and add the later effective date(s) for section 379A before re-running L3 review.

sections.title: Putting person in fear of harm in order to commit extortion
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The canonical text expressly requires caning, but the encoding preserves that punishment only in `supplementary` prose instead of as a structured `caning` penalty fact.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the prose-only caning sentence with an explicit caning punishment entry, using an unspecified form if the statute gives no stroke count.

sections.title: Putting person in fear of death or of grievous hurt in order to commit extortion
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The encoding omits a structured caning punishment even though the canonical text expressly requires punishment "with caning", and the repo now supports `caning := unspecified` for non-numeric caning terms.
sections.suggested_fix: Add an explicit `caning := unspecified` punishment entry so the mandatory caning limb is preserved structurally instead of only in supplementary prose.

sections.title: Several persons engaged in the commission of a criminal act may be guilty of different offences
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: The operative rule in the canonical text is flattened into two standalone definitions, so the section’s conjunctive "Where ..., they may ..." proposition is not actually encoded as a rule.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode the main clause as a structured rule or elements block that preserves the conditional relationship, while keeping the illustration unchanged.

sections.title: Attempt to commit robbery
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The encoding preserves the mandatory caning limb only in `supplementary` prose even though the canonical text expressly requires punishment with caning of not less than 6 strokes.
sections.suggested_fix: Add an explicit caning punishment entry and keep the statutory minimum-strokes language in supplementary text so the caning limb is preserved structurally without inventing a ceiling.

sections.title: Punishment for belonging to gang-robbers
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The encoded penalty omits the canonical life-imprisonment branch and adds a structured caning maximum of 24 strokes even though the statute only states not less than 6 strokes.
sections.suggested_fix: Re-encode the punishment so the life-imprisonment alternative is explicit and the caning clause preserves only the statutory minimum unless another canonical source supplies a maximum.

sections.title: Dishonest misappropriation of property possessed by a deceased person at the time of his death
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: The encoding introduces an `any_of` split between an invented `ordinary_case` and `clerk_or_servant_case`, but the canonical text states one offence with a higher imprisonment ceiling if the offender was the deceased's clerk or servant.
sections.suggested_fix: Remove the fabricated offence-level disjunction and model the clerk-or-servant status only as the predicate for the enhanced penalty branch.

sections.title: Criminal breach of trust of property entrusted for purposes of transportation or storage
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The penalty block makes the fine optional by nesting it under `alternative {}` even though the canonical text requires imprisonment and fine cumulatively ("and shall also be liable to fine").
sections.suggested_fix: Encode the fine as a direct cumulative penalty alongside the imprisonment term, without an `alternative {}` wrapper.

sections.title: “Offence”
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: Subsection (2) is not faithfully preserved because the encoded statutory text appends the non-canonical amendment marker "[15/2019; 2/2020]", and the section’s effective clauses do not cleanly reflect the `[15/2019; 2/2020]` amendment history.
sections.suggested_fix: Remove amendment markers from the quoted subsection text and encode the amendment chronology only in metadata/effective clauses.

sections.title: Stolen property
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is tagged `[15/2019]` but the encoding uses `effective 2019-12-31`, while repo-local L3 precedent for Act 15 of 2019 commencement is `2020-01-01`, so the effective-date encoding is not sane enough to stamp.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the later effective date with `2020-01-01` if that is the intended commencement date for this provision.

sections.title: Assisting in concealment or disposal of stolen property
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: The encoding uses an unsound `[15/2019]` effective date (`2019-12-31` instead of repo-local `2020-01-01`) and alters subsection (2)(b) by substituting `Road Traffic Act (Cap. 276)` for the canonical `Road Traffic Act 1961`.
sections.suggested_fix: Update the amendment effective date to the verified commencement date and restore subsection (2)(b) to the canonical Road Traffic Act wording before rerunning L3 review.

sections.title: Illegally obtained personal information
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: Subsections (2), (3), and (7) drop the canonical paragraph content and subsection (3)'s conjunctive structure, and the section uses `effective 2019-12-31` for `[15/2019]` despite repo-local L3 precedent treating Act 15 of 2019 commencement as `2020-01-01`.
sections.suggested_fix: Restore the missing `(2)(a)` to `(2)(b)`, `(3)(a)` to `(3)(b)`, and `(7)(a)` to `(7)(b)` content with the correct logical structure, then correct the amendment effective date.

sections.title: Cheating by remote communication
sections.rationale: subsection structure needs comparison
sections.reason: The encoding does not faithfully preserve subsection (2), omitting paragraphs (a) to (d) and the exclusion clause after "means communication through —".
sections.suggested_fix: Re-encode subsection (2) with all paragraph items and the full exclusion text from the canonical statute.

sections.title: Obtaining services dishonestly or fraudulently
sections.rationale: subsection structure needs comparison
sections.reason: The canonical entry includes subsection (1)(c) plus sub-items (c)(i) and (c)(ii), but the encoding omits them and therefore does not fully preserve subsection structure/content.
sections.suggested_fix: Re-encode paragraph (c) and its two sub-items as part of subsection (1) without changing the canonical wording.

sections.title: Fraud by false representation, non-disclosure or abuse of position not connected with contracts for goods or services
sections.rationale: subsection structure needs comparison
sections.reason: Subsection (5) is not fully preserved because the encoding omits the chapeau that its definitions apply "for the purposes of this section and section 424B", which drops a substantive cross-section scope statement.
sections.suggested_fix: Preserve subsection (5)'s introductory scope text explicitly in the encoding so the definitions are not represented as applying only within section 424A.

sections.title: Punishment for committing mischief
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is marked `[15/2019]` but the encoding uses only `effective 1872-01-01`, so the effective-date encoding is not sane enough to stamp.
sections.suggested_fix: Add the verified post-2019 commencement date for the Act 15 of 2019 amendment as an additional `effective` clause, then rerun L3 review.

sections.title: Punishment for the mischief described in section 437 when committed by fire or any explosive substance
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The encoding leaves the statute’s imprisonment-for-life alternative only in supplementary prose instead of preserving it as a structured penalty branch, so the punishment is not faithfully captured.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode imprisonment for life as an explicit structured alternative alongside the up-to-10-years branch, while retaining the conditional unlimited fine for non-life sentences.

sections.title: House-breaking
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is marked `[15/2019]` but the encoding only has `effective 1872-01-01`, so the later amendment effective date is missing and the effective-date encoding is not sane enough to stamp.
sections.suggested_fix: Add the applicable later effective date for the 2019 amendment to the statute header, consistent with the canonical amendment history and nearby amended sections.

sections.title: Punishment for criminal trespass
sections.rationale: penalty discrepancy needs side-by-side comparison
sections.reason: The canonical text makes the fine discretionary up to $1,500, but the encoding fixes it at exactly `$1,500.00`, which fabricates the penalty ceiling into a mandatory amount.
sections.suggested_fix: Change the fine encoding to a capped range that preserves "may extend to $1,500" rather than a fixed sum.

sections.title: Punishment for house-breaking
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is tagged `[15/2019]` but the encoding uses `effective 2019-12-31`, which repo-local L3 precedent already treats as an unsound commencement date for Act 15 of 2019.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the amendment effective date with the correct Act 15 of 2019 commencement date before resubmitting for L3 review.

sections.title: House-breaking in order to commit an offence punishable with death
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: The encoding does not preserve the imprisonment-for-life alternative as a structured penalty branch, and it uses `effective 2019-12-31` for a section tagged `[15/2019]`, which is not a reliable commencement date for that amendment.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode imprisonment for life as an explicit alternative penalty branch and replace the amendment effective date with the correct commencement date for Act 15 of 2019 before resubmitting for L3 review.

sections.title: House-breaking in order to commit an offence punishable with imprisonment for life
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is tagged `[15/2019]` but the encoding uses `effective 2019-12-31`, while repo-local L3 precedent treats Act 15 of 2019 commencement as `2020-01-01`, so the effective-date encoding is not sane enough to stamp.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the unsound 2019 amendment effective date with the correct commencement date used consistently for Act 15 of 2019 in this repo, then re-run L3 review.

sections.title: Possession of house-breaking implements or offensive weapons
sections.rationale: subsection structure needs comparison
sections.reason: Subsection (3) in the canonical text includes operative paragraphs (a) to (d), but the encoding preserves only the lead-in presumption sentence and drops all four categories.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode subsection (3)(a) to (d) explicitly instead of reducing subsection (3) to a single definition line.

sections.title: House-breaking when death or grievous hurt caused
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: The encoding flattens the statute's disjunctive aggravated-act phrase ("causes or attempts to cause death or grievous hurt") into a single element instead of modeling the alternatives with `any_of`.
sections.suggested_fix: Refine the aggravated-act limb into explicit disjunctive branches so the connective structure matches the English text.

sections.title: Forgery for the purpose of cheating
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: The encoding uses `penalty or_both`, but the canonical text makes imprisonment and liability to fine conjunctive, not disjunctive.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the disjunctive penalty form with the standard conjunctive penalty encoding used for sections that say the offender "shall also be liable to fine".

sections.title: Making or possessing equipment for making a false instrument
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoding uses only `effective 1872-01-01`, but section 473A is a later inserted provision and should include its later commencement date instead of only the original 1872 date.
sections.suggested_fix: Confirm the insertion commencement from the Penal Code legislative history and add that date as an `effective` clause for section 473A.

sections.title: Making or possessing equipment for making a false instrument with intent to induce prejudice
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: The English in paragraph (b) requires conjunctive intent content in both `(b)(i)` and `(b)(ii)`, but the encoding stops at `mens_rea intent_clause := "Intends that —";` and omits those operative limbs entirely.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode paragraph `(b)` so the intent element explicitly captures both `(b)(i)` and `(b)(ii)` as conjunctive requirements instead of a placeholder clause.

sections.title: Meaning of “prejudice” and “induce”
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: Subsection (1) encodes the `(a)` to `(f)` alternatives as opaque definition strings instead of preserving their disjunctive structure, so the English "or" logic is not represented faithfully.
sections.suggested_fix: Refine subsection `(1)` into structured alternatives that preserve the `(a)` to `(f)` list and its disjunctive connective.

sections.title: Counterfeiting a device or mark used for authenticating documents or electronic records other than those described in section 467, or possessing counterfeit marked material
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: The canonical penalty is imprisonment up to 10 years and liability to fine, but the encoding uses `penalty or_both`, which introduces an alternative fine-only branch not stated in the statute.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the penalty form with a cumulative or otherwise explicitly mandatory imprisonment-plus-fine encoding that matches the canonical text.

sections.title: Falsification of accounts
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoding omits the amendment commencement date `2022-03-01` from `[Act 23 of 2021 wef 01/03/2022]`, so the effective clauses are incomplete.
sections.suggested_fix: Add `effective 2022-03-01` to the statute header, preserving any already-valid commencement dates.

sections.title: Forging or counterfeiting currency or bank notes
sections.rationale: subsection structure needs comparison
sections.reason: Subsection (2) is not faithfully preserved because the encoding drops the statutory definitions of "bank note", "coin", and "currency" and keeps only the lead-in.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode subsection (2) with all three canonical definitions from `_raw/act.json` instead of the placeholder lead-in-only definition.

sections.title: Using as genuine forged or counterfeit currency or bank notes
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is tagged `[15/2019]`, but the encoding uses `effective 2019-12-31` and the repo contains conflicting guidance on whether Act 15 of 2019 commenced on `2019-12-31` or `2020-01-01`, so the effective-date encoding is not safe to stamp.
sections.suggested_fix: Confirm the commencement date for Act 15 of 2019 from an authoritative source, then update the second `effective` clause and re-run L3 review.

sections.title: Punishments
sections.rationale: check whether canonical explanations are present as `///` comments or structured refinements
sections.reason: The canonical explanation ("Explanation. —Caning shall be with a rattan.") is retained only as a bare definition string rather than as an explicit explanation/refinement form.
sections.suggested_fix: Re-encode the explanation as a labelled `/// Explanation` comment or another explicit explanation/refinement construct without changing the statute text.

sections.title: Application of enhanced penalties
sections.rationale: subsection structure needs comparison
sections.reason: Canonical subsection (2) contains operative limbs (a) and (b), but the encoding preserves only the introductory phrase and omits both substantive rules.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode subsection (2)(a) and (2)(b) as structured content under `subsection (2)` so the statutory rule is fully preserved.

sections.title: Act done by person bound, or justified by law
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoding includes amendment marker `[15/2019]` but only declares `effective 1872-01-01`, so it omits a later effective date required by the checklist.
sections.suggested_fix: Add the correct post-amendment effective date for the `[15/2019]` revision as an `effective` clause in `statute.yh`.

sections.title: Act done pursuant to the judgment or order of a court of justice
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: The encoding treats the “notwithstanding the court may have had no jurisdiction” clause as a required conjunctive circumstance, even though the canonical text makes it a non-limiting proposition.
sections.suggested_fix: Remove that clause from the mandatory `all_of` conditions and preserve it only as a non-required qualification while keeping the good-faith jurisdiction belief as the operative condition.

sections.title: Accident in the doing of a lawful act
sections.rationale: subsection structure needs comparison
sections.reason: Subsection (2) is not faithfully preserved because the encoding drops items (a) and (b) and the sentence requiring the prosecution to prove the fault element.
sections.suggested_fix: Re-encode subsection (2) so both conjunctive limbs and the prosecution-proof requirement are explicitly represented within the subsection block.

sections.title: Act of person of unsound mind
sections.rationale: no machine-readable check code; needs human read
sections.reason: Subsection (2) is reduced to a truncated definition string and omits the canonical (2)(a) and (2)(b) limbs, so the statute's required conjunctive structure is not faithfully encoded.
sections.suggested_fix: Encode subsection (2) as operative structure that captures both (2)(a) and (2)(b) as cumulative conditions instead of leaving only the introductory clause.

sections.title: Consent given under fear or misconception, by person of unsound mind, etc., and by child
sections.rationale: connective mismatch unclear without close read
sections.reason: The encoding flattens the section into quoted definition strings and does not represent the operative disjunctive limbs in canonical paragraphs (a)(i), (a)(ii), (b), and (c), so the English connective structure is not faithfully encoded.
sections.suggested_fix: Re-encode section 90 with explicit nested logical branches for paragraph (a) and the alternative limbs in paragraphs (b) and (c) instead of storing them as freeform definitions.

sections.title: Act to which a person is compelled by threats
sections.rationale: check whether canonical explanations are present as `///` comments or structured refinements
sections.reason: The two canonical explanations are not preserved as explanations or structured refinements, and the encoding adds a substantive self-placement condition not present in the canonical `text` or `sub_items`.
sections.suggested_fix: Preserve Explanation 1 and Explanation 2 explicitly in explanatory form and remove unsupported substantive conditions unless they appear in the canonical source.

sections.title: Nothing done in private defence is an offence
sections.rationale: exception preservation needs canonical-vs-encoded comparison
sections.reason: The encoding fabricates an `exception private_defence` block even though the canonical section is a single operative sentence with no canonical exceptions or sub-items.
sections.suggested_fix: Re-encode section 96 as a direct faithful rule without introducing a non-canonical exception structure.

sections.title: Right of private defence of the body and of property
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The encoding is tagged with amendment marker `[15/2019]` but the statute header only has `effective 1872-01-01`, so the post-2019 amendment date is missing.
sections.suggested_fix: Add the 2019 amendment effective date used for the surrounding private-defence sections instead of leaving section 97 with only the original 1872 date.

sections.title: Right of private defence against act of person of unsound mind, etc.
sections.rationale: no amendment markers detected; flag reasoning unclear
sections.reason: The section is tagged `[15/2019]` but the encoding uses `effective 2019-12-31`, while Act 15 of 2019 entered into force on 2020-01-01, so the effective-date encoding is not sane.
sections.suggested_fix: Replace the later effective date with `2020-01-01` if that is the intended commencement date for this provision.
